generated_comment:
A: Arre bhau, aajkal company wale bhi ajeeb chalte hain. Nayi porgi ko kaam pe lo, kal ko bolenge period leave, phir maternity leave, itna sab deke kaam ka hisaab kaun karega?
B: Haan re.
A: Mard log ko toh aisa kuch nahi milta. Phir appraisal aur project mein barabari kaise bole? Company ka nuksan hi hai na.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it frames women as workplace liabilities due to menstruation and maternity, and presents accommodations as unfair special treatment compared with men. It encodes fairness/cost arguments and the assumption that hiring women creates productivity and appraisal disadvantages.
original_frame: Prenup, Alimony as Reverse Dowry, Women
persona_text: You are Ramesh. Age 55. Married. A farmer in rural Maharashtra. Your neighbour's son just got divorced after two years of marriage and the wife took the gold jewellery and went back to her parents. You are talking to three other farmers sitting under a tree in the afternoon. You are commenting on what happened and connecting it to a broader pattern you have observed about what women from certain kinds of families are like and why young men today should be very careful before marrying
